author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew II. 4, 5.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he demanded of them where Christ should be born.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they said unto him, in Bethlehem of Judæa.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how all things are done to convict the Jews? how, as long as He was out of their sight, the envy had not yet laid hold of them, and they rehearsed the testimonies of Him with truth; but when they saw the glory that arose from the miracles, a grudging spirit possessed them, and thenceforth they betrayed the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, the truth was exalted by all things, and strength was the more gathered for it even by its enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See for example in this very case, how wonderful and beyond expectation are the results secretly provided for.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both the barbarians and the Jews do the same time alike learn something more of one another, and teach one another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus the Jews, for their part, heard from the wise men, that a star also had proclaimed Him in the land of the Persians; the wise men, in their turn, were informed by the Jews that this Man, whom the star proclaimed, prophets also had made known from a long time of old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the groundof their inquiry was made to both an occasion of setting forth clearer and more perfect instruction; and the enemies of the truth are compelled even against their will to read the writings in favor of the truth, and to interpret the prophecy; although not all of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having spoken of Bethlehem, and how that out of it He shall come that should rule Israel, they proceed not afterwards to add what follows, out of flattery to the king.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what was this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That “His goings forth are from of old, from everlasting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But why,” one may say, “if He was to come from thence, did He live in Nazareth after the birth, and obscure the prophecy?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, He did not obscure it, but unfolded it the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the fact, that while His mother had her constant residence in the one place, He was born in the other, shows the thing to have been done by a Divine dispensation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this cause, let me add, neither did He remove from thence straightway after His birth, but abode forty days, giving opportunity to them that were disposed to be inquisitive to examine all things accurately.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because there were in truth many things to move them to such an inquiry, at least if they had been disposed to give heed to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus at the coming of the wise men the whole city was in a flutter,and together with the city the king, and the prophet was brought forward, and a court of high authority was summoned; and many other things too were done there, all which Luke relates minutely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such were what concerns Anna, and Simeon, and Zacharias, and the angels, and the shepherds; all which things were to the attentive sufficient to give hints for ascertaining what had taken place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the wise men, who came from Persia, were not ignorant of the place, much more might they, whose abode it was, acquaint themselves with these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He manifested Himself then from the beginning by many miracles, but when they would not see, He hid Himself for a while, to be again revealed from another more glorious beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was no longer the wise men, nor the star, but the Father from above that proclaimed Him at the streams of Jordan; and the Spirit likewise came upon Him, guiding that voice to the head of Him just baptized; and John, with all plainness of speech, cried out everywhere in Judæa, till inhabited and waste country alike were filled with that kind of doctrine; and the witness too of the miracles, and earth, and sea, and the whole creation, uttered in His behalf a distinct voice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But at the time of the birth, just so many things happened as were fitted quietly to mark out Him that was come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, in order that the Jews might not say, “We know not when He was born, nor whereabouts,” both all these events in which the wise men were concerned were brought about by God’s providence, and the rest of the things which we have mentioned; so that they would have no excuse to plead, for not having inquired into that which had come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark also the exactness of the prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it does not say, “He will abide” in Bethlehem, but “He will come out” thence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that this too was a subject of prophecy, His being simply born there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Some of them, however, being past shame, say that these things were spoken of Zerubbabel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how can they be right?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely “his goings forth” were not “from of old, from everlasting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how can that suit him which is said at the beginning, “Out of thee shall He come forth:” Zorobabel not having been born in Judæa, but in Babylon, whence also he was called Zorobabel,because he had his origin there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as many as know the Syrians’ language know what I say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with what hath been said, all the time also since these things is sufficient to establish the testimony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou art not the least among the princes of Judah,” and he adds the cause of the pre-eminence, saying, “out of thee shall He come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But no one else hath made that place illustrious or eminent, excepting Him alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For example: since that birth, men come from the ends of the earth to see the manger, and the site of the shed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this the prophet foretold aloud from the first, saying, “Thou art not the least among the princes of Judah;” that is, among the heads of tribes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By which expression he comprehended even Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even so have they given heed, although the advantage passes on to themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and because of this the prophets at the beginning discourse nowhere so much of His dignity, as touching the benefit which accrued to them by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so, when the Virgin was bearing the child, he saith, “Thou shalt call His name Jesus;”and he gives the reason saying, “for He shall save His people from their sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the wise men too said not, “Where is the Son of God?” but “He that is born King of the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here again it is not affirmed, “Out of thee shall come forth” the Son of God, but “a Governor, that shall feed my people Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was needful to converse with them at first, setting out in a tone of very exceeding condescension, lest they should be offended; and to preach what related to their salvation in particular, that hereby they might be the rather won over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At any rate, all the testimonies that are first cited, and for which it was the season immediately at the time of the birth, say nothing great, nor lofty concerning Him, nor such as those subsequent to the manifestation of the miracles; for these discourse more distinctly concerning His dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For instance, when after many miracles children were singing hymns unto Him, hear what saith the prophet, “Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings Thou hast perfected praise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “I will consider the Heavens, the works of Thy fingers;” which signifies Him to be Maker of the universe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the testimony too, which was produced after the ascension, manifests His equality with the Father; thus saying, “The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on my right hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Isaiah too saith, “He that riseth up to rule over the Gentiles, in Him shall the Gentiles trust.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how saith he that Bethlehem is “not the least among the princes of Judah?” for not in Palestine alone, but in the whole world, the village hath become conspicuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, so far he was speaking to Jews; wherefore also he added, “He shall feed my people Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet He fed the whole world; but as I have said, He is fain not to offend as yet, by revealing what He hath to say touching the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how was it, one may say, that He did not feed the Jewish people?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I answer, first, this too is accomplished: for by the term Israel in this place, he figuratively meant such as believed on Him from among the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul interpreting this, saith, “For they are not all Israel, which are of Israel,”but as many as have been born by faith and promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if He did not feed them all, this is their own fault and blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they ought to have worshipped with the wise men, and have glorified God that such a time was come, doing away all their sins (for not a word was spoken to them of judgments set, or of accounts to be given, but of a mild and meek Shepherd); they for their part do just the contrary, and are troubled, and make disturbance, and go on continually framing plots without end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then Herod, when he had privily called the wise men, inquired of them diligentlywhat time the star appeared:”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Attempting to slay that which was born,—an act of extreme idiotcynot of madness only; since what had been said and done was enough to have withholden him from any such attempt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For those occurrences were not after the manner of man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A star, I mean, calling the wise men from on high; and barbarians making so long a pilgrimage, to worship Him that lay in swaddling clothes and a manger; and prophets too from of old, proclaiming beforehand all this;—these and all the rest were more than human events: but nevertheless, none of these things restrained him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such a thing is wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It falls foul of itself, and is ever attempting impossibilities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark his utter folly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If on the one hand he believed the prophecy, and accounted it to be unchangeable, it was quite clear that he was attempting impossibilities; if again he disbelieved, and did not expect that those sayings would come to pass, he need not have been in fear and alarm, nor have formed any plot on that behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that in either way his craft was superfluous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this too came of the utmost folly, to think that the wise men would make more account of him than of the Child that was born, for the sake of which they had come so long a journey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if, before they saw, they were so inflamed with longing for Him; after they had seen with their eyes, and been confirmed by the prophecy, how hoped he to persuade them to betray the young Child to him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, many as were the reasons to withhold him, he made the attempt; and having “privily called the wise men, he inquired of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he thought that Jews would be concerned in favor of the Child, and he never could expect that they would fall away unto such madness as to be willing to give up to His enemies their Protector and Saviour, and Him who was come for the deliverance of their nation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On account of this he both calls them privily, and seeks the time not of the Child, but of the star: thereby marking out the object of his chase so as to include far more than it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the star, I think, must have appeared a long time before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was a long time which the wise men had to spend on their journey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order, therefore, that they might present themselves just after His birth (it being meet for Him to be worshipped in His very swaddling clothes, that the marvellous and strange nature of the thing might appear), the star, a long time before, makes itself visible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereas if at the moment of His birth in Palestine, and not before, it had been seen by them in the East, they, consuming a long time in their journey, would not have seen Him in swaddling clothes on their arrival.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As to his slaying the children “from two years old and under,” let us not marvel; for his wrath and dread, for the sake of a fuller security, added very much to the time, so that not one might escape.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having therefore called them, he saith, “Go and search diligentlyfor the young Child; and when ye have found Him, bring me word again, that I may come and worship Him also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou his extreme folly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if thou sayest these things in sincerity, wherefore dost thou inquire privily?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if intending to plot against Him, how is it thou dost not perceive, that from the fact of their being asked secretly the wise men will be able to perceive thy craft?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as I have already said, a soul taken captive by any wickedness becomes more utterly senseless than any thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he said not, “go and learn concerning the King,” but “concerning the young Child;” for he could not even endure to call Him by the name of His dominion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the wise men perceive nothing of this, by reason of their exceeding reverence (for they never could have expected that he could have gone on to so great wickedness, and would have attempted to form plots against a dispensation so marvellous): and they depart suspecting none of these things, but from what was in themselves auguring all that would be in the rest of mankind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And, lo! the star, which they saw in the east, went before them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For therefore only was it hidden, that having lost their guide, they might come to be obliged to make inquiry of the Jews, and so the matter might be made evident to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since after they have made inquiries, and have had His enemiesfor informants, it appears to them again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark how excellent was the order; how in the first place after the star the peopleof the Jews receives them, and the king, and these bring in the prophecy to explain what had appeared: how next, after the prophet, an angel again took them up and taught them all things; but for a time they journey from Jerusalem to Bethlehem by the guidance of the star, the star again journeying with them from that place also; that hence too thou mightest learn, that this was not one of the ordinary stars, for there is not so much as one star that hath this nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it not merely moved, but “went before them,” drawing and guiding them on in mid-day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But what need of this star any more,” one may ask, “when the place was ascertained?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order that the Child also might be seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there was not anything to make Him manifest, since the house was not conspicuous, neither was His mother glorious, or distinguished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There was need then of the star, to set them by the place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore it re-appears on their coming out of Jerusalem, and stays not, before it hath reached the manger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And marvel was linked on to marvel; for both were strange things, as well the magi worshipping, as the star going before them; and enough to attract even such as were made all of stone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the wise men had said, they had heard prophets say these things, or that angels had discoursed with them in private, they might have been disbelieved; but now, when the vision of the star appeared on high, even they that were exceeding shameless had their mouths stopped.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Moreover, the star, when it stood over the young Child, stayed its course again: which thing itself also was of a greater power than belongs to a star, now to hide itself, now to appear, and having appeared to stand still.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence they too received an increase of faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause they rejoiced also, that they had found what they were seeking, that they had proved messengers of truth, that not without fruit had they come so great a journey; so great a longing (so to speak) had they for Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For first it came and stood over His very head, showing that what is born is Divine; next standing there, it leads them to worship Him; being not simply barbarians, but the wiser sort amongst them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, with how great fitness the star appeared?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why; because even after the prophecy, and after the interpretation of the chief priests and scribes, they still had their minds turned towards it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shame upon Marcion, shame upon Paul of Samosata,for refusing to see what those wise men saw,—the forefathers of the Church; for I am not ashamed so to call them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let Marcion be ashamed, beholding God worshipped in the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let Paul be ashamed, beholding Him worshipped as not being merely a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As to His being in the flesh, that first is signified by the swaddling clothes and the manger; as to their not worshipping Him as a mere man, they declare it, by offering Him, at that unripe age, such gifts as were meet to be offered to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with them let the Jews also be ashamed, seeing themselves anticipated by barbarians and magi, whilst they submit not so much as to come after them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed what happened then was a type of the things to come, and from the very beginning it was shown that the Gentiles would anticipate their nation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But how was it,” one may ask, “that not at the beginning, but afterwards, He said, ‘Go ye, and make disciples of all nations’”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because the occurrence was a type, as I said, of the future, and a sort of declaration of it beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the natural order was that Jews should come unto Him first; but forasmuch as they of their own choice gave up their proper benefit, the order of things was inverted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since not even in this instance should the wise men have come before the Jews, nor should persons from so great a distance have anticipated those who were settled about the very city, nor should those who had heard nothing have preventedthem that were nurtured in so many prophecies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But because they were exceedingly ignorant of their own blessings, those from Persia anticipate those at Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this indeed is what Paul also saith: “It was necessary that the word of the Lord should first have been spoken to you, but seeing ye have judged yourselves unworthy, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even though before they did not obey, at any rate when they heard it from the wise men, they ought to have made all haste; but they would not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, while those are slumbering, these run before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then also follow the magi, let us separate ourselves from our barbarian customs, and make our distance therefrom great, that we may see Christ, since they too, had they not been far from their own country, would have missed seeing Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us depart from the things of earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so the wise men, while they were in Persia, saw but the star, but after they had departed from Persia, they beheld the Sun of Righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather, they would not have seen so much as the star, unless they had readily risen up from thence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then also rise up; though all men be troubled, let us run to the house of the young Child; though kings, though nations, though tyrants interrupt this our path, let not our desire pass away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so shall we thoroughly repel all the dangers that beset us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since these too, except they had seen the young Child, would not have escaped their danger from the king.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Before seeing the young Child, fears and dangers and troubles pressed upon them from every side; but after the adoration, it is calm and security; and no longer a star but an angel receives them, having become priests from the act of adoration; for we see that they offered gifts also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou therefore likewise leave the Jewish people, the troubled city, the blood-thirsty tyrant, the pomp of the world, and hasten to Bethlehem, where is thehouse of the spiritual Bread.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though thou be a shepherd, and come hither, thou wilt behold the young Child in an inn: though thou be a king, and approach not here, thy purple robe will profit thee nothing; though thou be one of the wise men, this will be no hindrance to thee; only let thy coming be to honor and adore, not to spurn the Son of God; only do this with trembling and joy: for it is possible for both of these to concur in one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But take heed that thou be not like Herod, and say, “that I may come and worship Him,” and when thou art come, be minded to slay Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For him do they resemble, who partake of the mysteries unworthily: it being said, that such a one “shall be guilty of the Body and Blood of the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes; for they have in themselves the tyrant who is grieved at Christ’s kingdom, him that is more wicked than Herod of old, even Mammon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he would fain have the dominion, and sends them that are his own to worship in appearance, but slaying while they worship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us fear then, lest at any time, while we have the appearance of suppliants and worshippers, we should in deed show forth the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let us cast everything out of our hands when we are to worship; though it be gold that we have, let us offer it unto him and not bury it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if those barbarians then offered it for honor, what will become of thee, not giving even to Him that hath need?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If those men journeyed so far to see Him newly born, what sort of excuse wilt thou have, not going out of thy way one alley’s length, that thou mayest visit Him sick or in bonds?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet when they are sick or in bonds, even our enemies have our pity; thine is denied even to thy Benefactor and Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they offered gold, thou hardly givest bread.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They saw the star and were glad, thou, seeing Christ Himself a stranger and naked, art not moved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which of you, for Christ’s sake, hath made so long a pilgrimage, you that have received countless benefits, as these barbarians, or rather, these wiser than the wisest philosophers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why say I, so long a journey?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, many of our women are so delicate, that they go not over so much as one crossing of the streets to behold Him on the spiritual manger,unless they can have mules to draw them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And others being able to walk, yet prefer to their attendance here, some a crowd of worldly business, some the theatres.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereas the barbarians accomplished so great a journey for His sake, before seeing Him; thou not even after thou hast seen Him dost emulate them, but forsakest Him after seeing Him, and runnest to see the stage player.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: (For I touch again on the same subjects, as I did also of late.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And seeing Christ lying in the manger, thou leavest Him, that thou mayest see women on the stage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What thunderbolts do not these things deserve?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, if any one were to leadthee into a palace, and show thee the king on his throne, wouldest thou indeed choose to see the theatre instead of those things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet even in the palace there is nothing to gain; but here a spiritual well of fire gushes up out of this table.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou leavest this, and runnest down to the theatre, to see women swimming, and nature put to open dishonor, leaving Christ sitting by the well?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes: for now, as of old, He sits down by the well, not discoursing to a Samaritan woman, but to a whole city.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or perchance now too with a Samaritan woman only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither now is any one with Him; but some with their bodies only, and some not even with these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nevertheless, He retires not, but remains, and asks of us to drink, not water, but holiness, for “His holy things He gives unto the holy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not water that He gives us from this fountain, but living blood; and it is indeed a symbol of death, but it is become the cause of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, leaving the fountain of blood, the awful cup, goest thy way unto the fountain of the devil, to see a harlot swim, and to suffer shipwreck of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that water is a sea of lasciviousness, not drowning bodies, but working shipwreck of souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas she swims with naked body, thou beholding, art sunk into the deep of lasciviousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the devil’s net; it sinks, not them that go down into the water itself, but them that sit above more than such as wallow therein; and it chokes them more grievously than Pharaoh, who was of old sunk in the sea with his horses and his chariots.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if souls could but be seen, I could show you many floating on these waters, like the bodies of the Egyptians at that time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is still more grievous is this, that they even call such utter destruction a delight, and they term the sea of perdition a channel for a pleasure voyage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely one might easier pass over in safety the Ægean or the Tuscan sea, than this spectacle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the first place, through a whole night the devil preoccupies their souls with the expectation of it; then having shown them the expected object, he binds them at once, and makes them captives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For think not, because thou hast not been joined unto the harlot, thou art clean from the sin; for in the purpose of thine heart thou hast done it all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if thou be taken by lust, thou hast kindled the flame up higher; if thou feel nothing at what thou seest, thou deservest a heavier charge, for being a scandal to others, by encouraging them in these spectacles, and for polluting thine own eye-sight, and together with thine eye-sight, thy soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, not merely to find fault, come let us devise a mode of correction too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then will the mode be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I would commit you to your own wives, that they may instruct you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is true, according to Paul’s law,you ought to be the teachers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since that order is reversed by sin, and the body has come to be above, and the head beneath, let us even take this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou art ashamed to have a woman for thy teacher, fly from sin, and thou wilt quickly be able to mount up on the throne which God hath given thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since so long as thou sinnest the Scripture sends thee not to a woman only, but even to things irrational, and those of the viler sort; yea, it is not ashamed to send thee who art honored with reason, as a disciple to the ant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Plainly this is no charge against the Scripture, but against them that so betray their own nobility of race.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then we will do likewise; and for the present we will commit thee to thy wife; but if thou despise her, we will send thee away to the school of the very brutes, and will point out to thee how many birds, fishes, four-footed beasts, and creeping things are found more honorable, and chaster than thou.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If now thou art ashamed, and dost blush at the comparison, mount up to thine own nobility, and fly the sea of hell, and the flood of fire, I mean the pool in the theatre.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this pool introduces to that sea, and kindles that abyss of flame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if “he that looketh on a woman to lust after her hath already committed adultery,”he who is forced even to see her naked, how doth he not become ten thousandfold a captive?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The flood in the days of Noah did not so utterly destroy the race of men as these swimming women drown all that are there with great disgrace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as to that rain, though it wrought indeed a death of the body, yet did it repress the wickedness of the soul; but this hath the contrary effect; while the bodies remain, it destroys the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And ye, when there is a question of precedence, claim to take place of the whole word, forasmuch as our city first crowned itself with the name of Christian;but in the competition of chastity, ye are not ashamed to be behind the rudest cities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Well,” saith one, “and what dost thou require us to do? to occupy the mountains, and become monks?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why it is this which makes me sigh, that ye think them alone to be properly concerned with decency and chastity; and yet assuredly Christ made His laws common to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, when He saith, “if any one look on a woman to lust after her,” He speaks not to the solitary, but to him also that hath a wife; since in fact that mount was at that time filled with all kinds of persons of that description.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Form then in thy mind an image of that amphitheatre, and hate thou this, which is the devil’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither do thou condemn the severity of my speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I neither “forbid to marry,”nor hinder thy taking pleasure; but I would have this be done in chastity, not with shame, and reproach, and imputations without end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I do not make it a law that you are to occupy the mountains and the deserts, but to be good and considerate and chaste, dwelling in the midst of the city.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in fact all our laws are common to the monks also, except marriage; yea rather, even with respect to this, Paul commands us to put ourselves altogether on a level with them; saying, “For the fashion of this world passeth away:” that “they that have wives be as though they had none.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Wherefore” (so he speaks) “I do not bid you take possession of the summits of the mountains; it is true I could wish it, since the cities imitate the things that were done in Sodom; nevertheless, I do not enforce this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Abide, having house and children and wife; only do not insult thy wife, nor put thy children to shame, neither bring into thine house the infection from the theatre.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hearest thou not Paul saying, “The husband hath not power of his own body, but the wife,”and setting down laws common to both?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, if thy wife be continually thrusting herself into a public assembly, art severe in blaming her; but thyself, spending whole days on public shows, thou dost not account worthy of blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, touching thy wife’s modesty thou art so strict as even to go beyond necessity or measure, and not to allow her so much as indispensable absences; but to thyself thou deemest all things lawful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet Paul allows thee not, who gives the wife likewise the same authority, for thus he speaks: “Let the husband render unto the wife due honor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sort of honor then is this, when thou insultest her in the chiefest things, and givest up her body to harlots (for thy body is hers); when thou bringest tumults and wars into thine house, when thou doest in the market place such things, as being related by thyself to thy wife at home, overwhelm her with shame, and put to shame also thy daughter if present, and more than them, surely, thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou must necessarily either be silent, or behave thyself so unseemly, that it would be just for thy very servants to be scourged for it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What plea then wilt thou have, I pray thee, beholding, as thou dost, with great eagerness, things which even to name is disgraceful; preferring to all sights these, which even to recount is intolerable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now then for a season, in order not to be too burdensome, I will here bring my discourse to an end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye continue in the same courses, I will make the knife sharper, and the cut deeper; and I will not cease, till I have scattered the theatre of the devil, and so purified the assembly of the Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in this way we shall both be delivered from the present disgrace, and shall reap the fruit of the life to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might for ever and ever.
